questions.question: What signal does Toolformer use to decide whether to keep a candidate tool annotation?
options: Whether including the tool result reduces next-token loss on surrounding text
Whether the tool returns within 100 ms
Whether the tool emits valid JSON
questions.explanation: Toolformer's self-supervised signal keeps annotations whose tool results lower next-token loss.

questions.question: What is the BFCL V4 split that the lesson reports?
questions.explanation: BFCL V4 weights are 40 agentic / 30 multi-turn / 10 live / 10 non-live / 10 hallucination.

questions.question: Why is a tool's description load-bearing?
options: It is what the user sees in the UI
The model reads it to choose the right tool; bad descriptions are the top cause of wrong-tool failures
It is required by JSON Schema
questions.explanation: Tool descriptions are the model's primary signal for tool selection; poor descriptions cause wrong-tool routing.

questions.question: What role does tool_use_id play in parallel tool calling?
options: It correlates each tool result with its originating call so results returning out of order route correctly
It authorizes the call against an API key
questions.explanation: tool_use_id is the correlation token; swapping them routes results to the wrong call.

questions.question: Which class of failure does V3 state-based evaluation try to catch?
options: AST-matching tool calls that look right but leave the API in the wrong state
questions.explanation: State-based evaluation checks the resulting API state (e.g. file actually created) rather than syntactic call matching.

questions.question: Which 2026 problem does the lesson NOT list among the open ones for function calling?
questions.explanation: The open problems are memory, dynamic decision-making, long-horizon chains, and hallucination detection; decoding speed is not on the list.

questions.question: Why is a generic run_shell(cmd) tool called a red flag in this lesson?
options: It widens the sandbox boundary; specific tools like git_status() bound read/write surface and risk
It cannot be called in parallel
questions.explanation: Narrow tools constrain the sandbox surface; a generic shell tool grants the full surface of the host.
